Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.description: Caused by Xanthomonas species. Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and raised scab-like spots on fruit.
treatment: Avoid overhead irrigation and working in wet fields
Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years

Potato___Early_blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Dark brown spots with concentric rings (target spots) on lower leaves first.
treatment: Apply Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb fungicides preventatively

Potato___Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Water-soaked lesions that rapidly enlarge. The disease that caused the Irish Potato Famine.
treatment: Apply Metalaxyl or Dimethomorph fungicides immediately
This is an emergency - act fast to prevent spread

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.description: Caused by Xanthomonas species. Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and raised spots on fruit.
treatment: Rotate with non-solanaceous crops for 2-3 years

Tomato_Early_blight.description: Caused by Alternaria solani. Dark concentric-ringed spots starting on older leaves, working upward.
treatment: Stake plants for better air circulation

Tomato_Late_blight.description: Caused by Phytophthora infestans. Large, irregularly shaped, water-soaked lesions. Can destroy entire crops rapidly.
treatment: Remove and destroy all infected plants

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.description: Caused by Passalora fulva. Pale yellow spots on upper leaf surface, olive-green mold on lower surface.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.description: Caused by Septoria lycopersici. Numerous small, circular spots with dark borders and gray centers.
treatment: Remove and destroy infected lower leaves

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.description: Tiny arachnids that feed on leaf undersides causing stippling, yellowing, and webbing.
treatment: Spray plants with strong water jets to dislodge mites
Use miticides like Abamectin or Spiromesifen
Avoid broad-spectrum insecticides that kill natural predators

Tomato__Target_Spot.description: Caused by Corynespora cassiicola. Concentric-ringed brown spots on leaves, stems, and fruit.
treatment: Improve plant spacing for air circulation
Remove and destroy affected plant parts

Tomato__Tomato_YellowLeaf__Curl_Virus.description: A viral disease transmitted by whiteflies. Causes severe leaf curling, yellowing, and stunted growth.
treatment: Control whiteflies with Imidacloprid or yellow sticky traps
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Use reflective mulch to repel whiteflies

Tomato__Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: A viral disease causing mosaic patterns (mottled light/dark green) on leaves and reduced fruit quality.
treatment: No cure exists - remove and destroy infected plants
